ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Ta, Seen, Meem.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ṭâ-Sĩn-Mĩm.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: These are the verses of the clear Book.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: These are the verses of the clear Book.

ayahs.textArabic: لَعَلَّكَ بَٰخِعٞ نَّفۡسَكَ أَلَّا يَكُونُواْ مُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: LaAAallaka bakhiAAun nafsaka alla yakoonoo mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Perhaps, [O Muhammad], you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Perhaps you ˹O Prophet˺ will grieve yourself to death over their disbelief.

ayahs.textArabic: إِن نَّشَأۡ نُنَزِّلۡ عَلَيۡهِم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ءَايَةٗ فَظَلَّتۡ أَعۡنَٰقُهُمۡ لَهَا خَٰضِعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In nasha nunazzil AAalayhim mina alssamai ayatan fathallat aAAnaquhum laha khadiAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: If We willed, We could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If We willed, We could send down upon them a ˹compelling˺ sign from the heavens, leaving their necks bent in ˹utter˺ submission to it.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا يَأۡتِيهِم مِّن ذِكۡرٖ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ مُحۡدَثٍ إِلَّا كَانُواْ عَنۡهُ مُعۡرِضِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama yateehim min thikrin mina alrrahmani muhdathin illa kanoo AAanhu muAArideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And no revelation comes to them anew from the Most Merciful except that they turn away from it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whatever new reminder comes to them from the Most Compassionate, they always turn away from it.

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَدۡ كَذَّبُواْ فَسَيَأۡتِيهِمۡ أَنۢبَٰٓؤُاْ مَا كَانُواْ بِهِۦ يَسۡتَهۡزِءُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqad kaththaboo fasayateehim anbao ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For they have already denied, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They have certainly denied ˹the truth˺, so they will soon face the consequences of their ridicule.

ayahs.textArabic: أَوَلَمۡ يَرَوۡاْ إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ كَمۡ أَنۢبَتۡنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوۡجٖ كَرِيمٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Awalam yaraw ila alardi kam anbatna feeha min kulli zawjin kareemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Did they not look at the earth - how much We have produced therein from every noble kind?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have they failed to look at the earth, ˹to see˺ how many types of fine plants We have caused to grow in it?

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبُّكَ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱئۡتِ ٱلۡقَوۡمَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waith nada rabbuka moosa ani iti alqawma alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] when your Lord called Moses, [saying], "Go to the wrongdoing people -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when your Lord called out to Moses, “Go to the wrongdoing people—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Allah?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear ˹Allah˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّيٓ أَخَافُ أَن يُكَذِّبُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi innee akhafu an yukaththibooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord, indeed I fear that they will deny me
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “My Lord! I fear that they will reject me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَضِيقُ صَدۡرِي وَلَا يَنطَلِقُ لِسَانِي فَأَرۡسِلۡ إِلَىٰ هَٰرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayadeequ sadree wala yantaliqu lisanee faarsil ila haroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Aaron.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹so˺ my heart will be broken and my tongue will be tied. So send Aaron along ˹as a messenger˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَهُمۡ عَلَيَّ ذَنۢبٞ فَأَخَافُ أَن يَقۡتُلُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walahum AAalayya thanbun faakhafu an yaqtulooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they have upon me a [claim due to] sin, so I fear that they will kill me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Also, they have a charge against me, so I fear they may kill me.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ كـَلَّاۖ فَٱذۡهَبَا بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآۖ إِنَّا مَعَكُم مُّسۡتَمِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala kalla faithhaba biayatina inna maAAakum mustamiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "No. Go both of you with Our signs; indeed, We are with you, listening.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah responded, “Certainly not! So go, both of you, with Our signs. We will be with you, listening.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأۡتِيَا فِرۡعَوۡنَ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatiya firAAawna faqoola inna rasoolu rabbi alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the messengers of the Lord of the worlds,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Go to Pharaoh and say, ‘We are messengers from the Lord of all worlds,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Commanded to say], "Send with us the Children of Israel."'"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹commanded to say:˺ ‘Let the Children of Israel go with us.’’”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ أَلَمۡ نُرَبِّكَ فِينَا وَلِيدٗا وَلَبِثۡتَ فِينَا مِنۡ عُمُرِكَ سِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala alam nurabbika feena waleedan walabithta feena min AAumurika sineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh protested, “Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you stayed several years of your life in our care?

ayahs.textArabic: وَفَعَلۡتَ فَعۡلَتَكَ ٱلَّتِي فَعَلۡتَ وَأَنتَ مِنَ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WafaAAalta faAAlataka allatee faAAalta waanta mina alkafireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [then] you did your deed which you did, and you were of the ungrateful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then you did what you did, being ˹utterly˺ ungrateful!”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَعَلۡتُهَآ إِذٗا وَأَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala faAAaltuha ithan waana mina alddalleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "I did it, then, while I was of those astray.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses replied, “I did it then, lacking guidance.

ayahs.textArabic: فَفَرَرۡتُ مِنكُمۡ لَمَّا خِفۡتُكُمۡ فَوَهَبَ لِي رَبِّي حُكۡمٗا وَجَعَلَنِي مِنَ ٱلۡمُرۡسَلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fafarartu minkum lamma khiftukum fawahaba lee rabbee hukman wajaAAalanee mina almursaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and prophethood and appointed me [as one] of the messengers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and made me one of the messengers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَتِلۡكَ نِعۡمَةٞ تَمُنُّهَا عَلَيَّ أَنۡ عَبَّدتَّ بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Watilka niAAmatun tamunnuha AAalayya an AAabbadta banee israeela
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: How can that be a ‘favour,’ of which you remind me, when ˹it was only because˺ you ˹have˺ enslaved the Children of Israel?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Said Pharaoh, "And what is the Lord of the worlds?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh asked, “And what is ‘the Lord of all worlds’?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَآۖ إِن كُنتُم مُّوقِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma in kuntum mooqineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "The Lord of the heavens and earth and that between them, if you should be convinced."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses replied, “˹He is˺ the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, if only you had sure faith.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said to those around him, "Do you not hear?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh said to those around him, “Did you hear ˹what he said˺?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "Your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses added, “˹He is˺ your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَكُمُ ٱلَّذِيٓ أُرۡسِلَ إِلَيۡكُمۡ لَمَجۡنُونٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala inna rasoolakumu allathee orsila ilaykum lamajnoonun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "Indeed, your 'messenger' who has been sent to you is mad."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh said ˹mockingly˺, “Your messenger, who has been sent to you, must be insane.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلۡمَشۡرِقِ وَٱلۡمَغۡرِبِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَآۖ إِن كُنتُمۡ تَعۡقِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbu almashriqi waalmaghribi wama baynahuma in kuntum taAAqiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "Lord of the east and the west and that between them, if you were to reason."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses responded: “˹He is˺ the Lord of the east and west, and everything in between, if only you had any sense.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ لَئِنِ ٱتَّخَذۡتَ إِلَٰهًا غَيۡرِي لَأَجۡعَلَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلۡمَسۡجُونِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala laini ittakhathta ilahan ghayree laajAAalannaka mina almasjooneena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "If you take a god other than me, I will surely place you among those imprisoned."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh threatened, “If you take any other god besides me, I will certainly have you imprisoned.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "Even if I brought you proof manifest?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses responded, “Even if I bring you a clear proof?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَأۡتِ بِهِۦٓ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala fati bihi in kunta mina alssadiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "Then bring it, if you should be of the truthful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh demanded, “Bring it then, if what you say is true.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَلۡقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ ثُعۡبَانٞ مُّبِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faalqa AAasahu faitha hiya thuAAbanun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So [Moses] threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent manifest.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So he threw down his staff and—behold!—it became a real snake.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُۥ فَإِذَا هِيَ بَيۡضَآءُ لِلنَّٰظِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WanazaAAa yadahu faitha hiya baydao lilnnathireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white for the observers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he drew his hand ˹out of his collar˺ and it was ˹shining˺ white for all to see.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ لِلۡمَلَإِ حَوۡلَهُۥٓ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَسَٰحِرٌ عَلِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala lilmalai hawlahu inna hatha lasahirun AAaleemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said to the eminent ones around him, "Indeed, this is a learned magician.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh said to the chiefs around him, “He is indeed a skilled magician,

ayahs.textArabic: يُرِيدُ أَن يُخۡرِجَكُم مِّنۡ أَرۡضِكُم بِسِحۡرِهِۦ فَمَاذَا تَأۡمُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yureedu an yukhrijakum min ardikum bisihrihi famatha tamuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic, so what do you advise?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who seeks to drive you from your land by his magic. So what do you propose?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ أَرۡجِهۡ وَأَخَاهُ وَٱبۡعَثۡ فِي ٱلۡمَدَآئِنِ حَٰشِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo arjih waakhahu waibAAath fee almadaini hashireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “Let him and his brother wait and dispatch mobilizers to all cities

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who will bring you every learned, skilled magician."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to bring you every skilled magician.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well-known day.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So the magicians were assembled at the set time on the appointed day.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it was said to the people, "Will you congregate
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And the people were asked, “Will you join the gathering,

ayahs.textArabic: لَعَلَّنَا نَتَّبِعُ ٱلسَّحَرَةَ إِن كَانُواْ هُمُ ٱلۡغَٰلِبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: LaAAallana nattabiAAu alssaharata in kanoo humu alghalibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so that we may follow the magicians if they prevail?”

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُواْ لِفِرۡعَوۡنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجۡرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحۡنُ ٱلۡغَٰلِبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma jaa alssaharatu qaloo lifirAAawna ainna lana laajran in kunna nahnu alghalibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the magicians came, they asked Pharaoh, “Shall we have a ˹suitable˺ reward if we prevail?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ نَعَمۡ وَإِنَّكُمۡ إِذٗا لَّمِنَ ٱلۡمُقَرَّبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala naAAam wainnakum ithan lamina almuqarrabeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “Yes, and you will then certainly be among those closest to me.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلۡقُواْ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلۡقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala lahum moosa alqoo ma antum mulqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses said to them, “Cast whatever you wish to cast.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَلۡقَوۡاْ حِبَالَهُمۡ وَعِصِيَّهُمۡ وَقَالُواْ بِعِزَّةِ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّا لَنَحۡنُ ٱلۡغَٰلِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faalqaw hibalahum waAAisiyyahum waqaloo biAAizzati firAAawna inna lanahnu alghaliboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, "By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they cast down their ropes and staffs, saying, “By Pharaoh’s might, it is we who will surely prevail.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَلۡقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ تَلۡقَفُ مَا يَأۡفِكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faalqa moosa AAasahu faitha hiya talqafu ma yafikoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then Moses threw down his staff, and—behold!—it devoured the objects of their illusion!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So the magicians fell down, prostrating.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They declared, “We ˹now˺ believe in the Lord of all worlds—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ قَبۡلَ أَنۡ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِي عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحۡرَ فَلَسَوۡفَ تَعۡلَمُونَۚ لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيۡدِيَكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُم مِّنۡ خِلَٰفٖ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala amantum lahu qabla an athana lakum innahu lakabeerukumu allathee AAallamakumu alssihra falasawfa taAAlamoona laoqattiAAanna aydiyakum waarjulakum min khilafin walaosallibannakum ajmaAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed Moses before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic, but you are going to know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh threatened, “How dare you believe in him before I give you permission? He must be your master who taught you magic, but soon you will see. I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then crucify you all.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ لَا ضَيۡرَۖ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo la dayra inna ila rabbina munqaliboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They responded, “˹That would be˺ no harm! Surely to our Lord we will ˹all˺ return.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا نَطۡمَعُ أَن يَغۡفِرَ لَنَا رَبُّنَا خَطَٰيَٰنَآ أَن كُنَّآ أَوَّلَ ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna natmaAAu an yaghfira lana rabbuna khatayana an kunna awwala almumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, we aspire that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We really hope that our Lord will forgive our sins, as we are the first to believe.”

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَأَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنۡ أَسۡرِ بِعِبَادِيٓ إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waawhayna ila moosa an asri biAAibadee innakum muttabaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants; indeed, you will be pursued."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We inspired Moses, ˹saying,˺ “Leave with My servants at night, for you will surely be pursued.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then Pharaoh sent mobilizers to all cities,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And said], "Indeed, those are but a small band,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹and said,˺ “These ˹outcasts˺ are just a handful of people,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, they are enraging us,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who have really enraged us,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, we are a cautious society... "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but we are all on the alert.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We removed them from gardens and springs
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We lured the tyrants out of ˹their˺ gardens, springs,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And treasures and honorable station -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: treasures, and splendid residences.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thus. And We caused to inherit it the Children of Israel.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So it was. And We awarded it ˹all˺ to the Children of Israel.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they pursued them at sunrise.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And so they pursued them at sunrise.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّا تَرَٰٓءَا ٱلۡجَمۡعَانِ قَالَ أَصۡحَٰبُ مُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّا لَمُدۡرَكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma taraa aljamAAani qala ashabu moosa inna lamudrakoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when the two companies saw one another, the companions of Moses said, "Indeed, we are to be overtaken!"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the two groups came face to face, the companions of Moses cried out, “We are overtaken for sure.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ كـَلَّآۖ إِنَّ مَعِيَ رَبِّي سَيَهۡدِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala kalla inna maAAiya rabbee sayahdeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "No! Indeed, with me is my Lord; He will guide me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses reassured ˹them˺, “Absolutely not! My Lord is certainly with me—He will guide me.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱضۡرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلۡبَحۡرَۖ فَٱنفَلَقَ فَكَانَ كُلُّ فِرۡقٖ كَٱلطَّوۡدِ ٱلۡعَظِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faawhayna ila moosa ani idrib biAAasaka albahra fainfalaqa fakana kullu firqin kaalttawdi alAAatheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We inspired to Moses, "Strike with your staff the sea," and it parted, and each portion was like a great towering mountain.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We inspired Moses: “Strike the sea with your staff,” and the sea was split, each part was like a huge mountain.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We advanced thereto the pursuers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We drew the pursuers to that place,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We saved Moses and those with him, all together.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and delivered Moses and those with him all together.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We drowned the others.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We drowned the others.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And recite to them the news of Abraham,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Relate to them ˹O Prophet˺ the story of Abraham,

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦ مَا تَعۡبُدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala liabeehi waqawmihi ma taAAbudoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When he said to his father and his people, "What do you worship?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when he questioned his father and his people, “What is that you worship ˹besides Allah˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ نَعۡبُدُ أَصۡنَامٗا فَنَظَلُّ لَهَا عَٰكِفِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo naAAbudu asnaman fanathallu laha AAakifeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "We worship idols and remain to them devoted."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “We worship idols, to which we are fully devoted.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Do they hear you when you supplicate?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Abraham asked, “Can they hear you when you call upon them?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they benefit you, or do they harm?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or can they benefit or harm you?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ بَلۡ وَجَدۡنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا كَذَٰلِكَ يَفۡعَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo bal wajadna abaana kathalika yafAAaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "But we found our fathers doing thus."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “No! But we found our forefathers doing the same.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Then do you see what you have been worshipping,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Abraham responded, “Have you ˹really˺ considered what you have been worshipping—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You and your ancient forefathers?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: you and your ancestors?

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِنَّهُمۡ عَدُوّٞ لِّيٓ إِلَّا رَبَّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fainnahum AAaduwwun lee illa rabba alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, they are enemies to me, except the Lord of the worlds,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They are ˹all˺ enemies to me, except the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who created me, and He [it is who] guides me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is˺ the One Who created me, and He ˹alone˺ guides me.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who feeds me and gives me drink.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is˺ the One Who provides me with food and drink.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when I am ill, it is He who cures me
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He ˹alone˺ heals me when I am sick.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And who will cause me to die and then bring me to life
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He ˹is the One Who˺ will cause me to die, and then bring me back to life.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِيٓ أَطۡمَعُ أَن يَغۡفِرَ لِي خَطِيٓـَٔتِي يَوۡمَ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallathee atmaAAu an yaghfira lee khateeatee yawma alddeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And who I aspire that He will forgive me my sin on the Day of Recompense."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is ˹the One˺ Who, I hope, will forgive my flaws on Judgment Day.”

ayahs.textArabic: رَبِّ هَبۡ لِي حُكۡمٗا وَأَلۡحِقۡنِي بِٱلصَّٰلِحِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbi hab lee hukman waalhiqnee bialssaliheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And he said], "My Lord, grant me authority and join me with the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “My Lord! Grant me wisdom, and join me with the righteous.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱجۡعَل لِّي لِسَانَ صِدۡقٖ فِي ٱلۡأٓخِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaijAAal lee lisana sidqin fee alakhireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And grant me a reputation of honor among later generations.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Bless me with honourable mention among later generations.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And place me among the inheritors of the Garden of Pleasure.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Make me one of those awarded the Garden of Bliss.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱغۡفِرۡ لِأَبِيٓ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waighfir liabee innahu kana mina alddalleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And forgive my father. Indeed, he has been of those astray.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Forgive my father, for he is certainly one of the misguided.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not disgrace me on the Day they are [all] resurrected -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And do not disgrace me on the Day all will be resurrected—

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ لَا يَنفَعُ مَالٞ وَلَا بَنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma la yanfaAAu malun wala banoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day when there will not benefit [anyone] wealth or children
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Day when neither wealth nor children will be of any benefit.

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا مَنۡ أَتَى ٱللَّهَ بِقَلۡبٖ سَلِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa man ata Allaha biqalbin saleemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But only one who comes to Allah with a sound heart."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Only those who come before Allah with a pure heart ˹will be saved˺.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Paradise will be brought near [that Day] to the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹On that Day˺ Paradise will be brought near to the God-fearing,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Hellfire will be brought forth for the deviators,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the Hellfire will be displayed to the deviant.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقِيلَ لَهُمۡ أَيۡنَ مَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡبُدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqeela lahum ayna ma kuntum taAAbudoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it will be said to them, "Where are those you used to worship
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And it will be said to them, “Where are those you used to worship

ayahs.textArabic: مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ هَلۡ يَنصُرُونَكُمۡ أَوۡ يَنتَصِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Min dooni Allahi hal yansuroonakum aw yantasiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Other than Allah? Can they help you or help themselves?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: besides Allah? Can they help you or even help themselves?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they will be overturned into Hellfire, they and the deviators
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then the idols will be hurled headlong into Hell, along with the deviant

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the soldiers of Iblees, all together.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the soldiers of Iblîs, all together.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say while they dispute therein,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There the deviant will cry while disputing with their idols,

ayahs.textArabic: تَٱللَّهِ إِن كُنَّا لَفِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: TaAllahi in kunna lafee dalalin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "By Allah, we were indeed in manifest error
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “By Allah! We were clearly mistaken,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When we equated you with the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when we made you equal to the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And no one misguided us except the criminals.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And none led us astray other than the wicked.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So now we have no intercessors
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now we have none to intercede for us,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And not a devoted friend.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: nor a close friend.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَوۡ أَنَّ لَنَا كَرَّةٗ فَنَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falaw anna lana karratan fanakoona mina almumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then if we only had a return [to the world] and could be of the believers... "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If only we could have a second chance, then we would be believers.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The people of Noah denied the messengers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The people of Noah rejected the messengers

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ أَخُوهُمۡ نُوحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohum noohun ala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When their brother Noah said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when their brother Noah said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍۖ إِنۡ أَجۡرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Should we believe you while you are followed by the lowest [class of people]?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They argued, “How can we believe in you, when you are followed ˹only˺ by the lowest of the low?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ وَمَا عِلۡمِي بِمَا كَانُواْ يَعۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala wama AAilmee bima kanoo yaAAmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "And what is my knowledge of what they used to do?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He responded, “And what knowledge do I have of what they do?

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ حِسَابُهُمۡ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّيۖ لَوۡ تَشۡعُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In hisabuhum illa AAala rabbee law tashAAuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Their account is only upon my Lord, if you [could] perceive.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Their judgment is with my Lord, if you had any sense!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I am not one to drive away the believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I am not going to expel the believers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I am only a clear warner."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I am only sent with a clear warning.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ لَئِن لَّمۡ تَنتَهِ يَٰنُوحُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلۡمَرۡجُومِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo lain lam tantahi ya noohu latakoonanna mina almarjoomeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They threatened, “If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be stoned ˹to death˺.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord, indeed my people have denied me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Noah prayed, “My Lord! My people have truly rejected me.

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱفۡتَحۡ بَيۡنِي وَبَيۡنَهُمۡ فَتۡحٗا وَنَجِّنِي وَمَن مَّعِيَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faiftah baynee wabaynahum fathan wanajjinee waman maAAiya mina almumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So judge between me and them decisively, and save me and the believers with me.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَنجَيۡنَٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِي ٱلۡفُلۡكِ ٱلۡمَشۡحُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faanjaynahu waman maAAahu fee alfulki almashhooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We saved him and those with him in the fully loaded Ark.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We drowned thereafter the remaining ones.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then afterwards We drowned the rest.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: 'Aad denied the messengers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The people of ’Âd rejected the messengers

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ أَخُوهُمۡ هُودٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohum hoodun ala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When their brother Hud said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when their brother Hûd said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍۖ إِنۡ أَجۡرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do you construct on every elevation a sign, amusing yourselves,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Why˺ do you build a landmark on every high place in vanity,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And take for yourselves palaces and fortresses that you might abide eternally?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and construct castles, as if you are going to live forever,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when you strike, you strike as tyrants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and act so viciously when you attack ˹others˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And fear He who provided you with that which you know,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Fear the One Who has provided you with ˹the good˺ things you know:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Provided you with grazing livestock and children
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He provided you with cattle, and children,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And gardens and springs.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and gardens, and springs.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنِّيٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيۡكُمۡ عَذَابَ يَوۡمٍ عَظِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innee akhafu AAalaykum AAathaba yawmin AAatheemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I truly fear for you the torment of a tremendous day.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيۡنَآ أَوَعَظۡتَ أَمۡ لَمۡ تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلۡوَٰعِظِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo sawaon AAalayna awaAAathta am lam takun mina alwaAAitheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "It is all the same to us whether you advise or are not of the advisors.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They responded, “It is all the same to us whether you warn ˹us˺ or not.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This is not but the custom of the former peoples,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is simply the tradition of our predecessors.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we are not to be punished."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And we will never be punished.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَهۡلَكۡنَٰهُمۡۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakaththaboohu faahlaknahum inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they denied him, so We destroyed them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they rejected him, and ˹so˺ We destroyed them. Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thamud denied the messengers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The people of Thamûd rejected the messengers

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ أَخُوهُمۡ صَٰلِحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohum salihun ala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When their brother Salih said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when their brother Ṣâliḥ said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍۖ إِنۡ أَجۡرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Will you be left in what is here, secure [from death],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do you think you will be ˹forever˺ left secure in what you have here:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Within gardens and springs
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: amid gardens and springs,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And fields of crops and palm trees with softened fruit?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹various˺ crops, and palm trees ˹loaded˺ with tender fruit;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you carve out of the mountains, homes, with skill.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to carve homes in the mountains with great skill?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not obey the order of the transgressors,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And do not follow the command of the transgressors,

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُفۡسِدُونَ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَلَا يُصۡلِحُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena yufsidoona fee alardi wala yuslihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who cause corruption in the land and do not amend."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who spread corruption throughout the land, never setting things right.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “You are simply bewitched!

ayahs.textArabic: مَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٞ مِّثۡلُنَا فَأۡتِ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma anta illa basharun mithluna fati biayatin in kunta mina alssadiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You are but a man like ourselves, so bring a sign, if you should be of the truthful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You are only a human being like us, so bring forth a sign if what you say is true.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ هَٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةٞ لَّهَا شِرۡبٞ وَلَكُمۡ شِرۡبُ يَوۡمٖ مَّعۡلُومٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala hathihi naqatun laha shirbun walakum shirbu yawmin maAAloomin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "This is a she-camel. For her is a [time of] drink, and for you is a [time of] drink, [each] on a known day.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ṣâliḥ said, “Here is a camel. She will have her turn to drink as you have yours, each on an appointed day.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٖ فَيَأۡخُذَكُمۡ عَذَابُ يَوۡمٍ عَظِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala tamassooha bisooin fayakhuthakum AAathabu yawmin AAatheemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not touch her with harm, lest you be seized by the punishment of a terrible day."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And do not ever touch her with harm, or you will be overtaken by the torment of a tremendous day.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they hamstrung her and so became regretful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But they killed her, becoming regretful.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلۡعَذَابُۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faakhathahumu alAAathabu inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the punishment seized them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So the punishment overtook them. Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The people of Lot denied the messengers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The people of Lot rejected the messengers

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ أَخُوهُمۡ لُوطٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala lahum akhoohum lootun ala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When their brother Lot said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when their brother Lot said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍۖ إِنۡ أَجۡرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do you approach males among the worlds
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Why do you ˹men˺ lust after fellow men,

ayahs.textArabic: وَتَذَرُونَ مَا خَلَقَ لَكُمۡ رَبُّكُم مِّنۡ أَزۡوَٰجِكُمۚ بَلۡ أَنتُمۡ قَوۡمٌ عَادُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Watatharoona ma khalaqa lakum rabbukum min azwajikum bal antum qawmun AAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And leave what your Lord has created for you as mates? But you are a people transgressing."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: leaving the wives that your Lord has created for you? In fact, you are a transgressing people.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ لَئِن لَّمۡ تَنتَهِ يَٰلُوطُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلۡمُخۡرَجِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo lain lam tantahi ya lootu latakoonanna mina almukhrajeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be of those evicted."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They threatened, “If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be expelled.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Indeed, I am, toward your deed, of those who detest [it].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Lot responded, “I am truly one of those who despise your ˹shameful˺ practice.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: My Lord, save me and my family from [the consequence of] what they do."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: My Lord! Save me and my family from ˹the consequences of˺ what they do.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We saved him and his family, all,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We saved him and all of his family,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except an old woman among those who remained behind.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except an old woman, who was one of the doomed.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We destroyed the others.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We utterly destroyed the rest,

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمۡطَرۡنَا عَلَيۡهِم مَّطَرٗاۖ فَسَآءَ مَطَرُ ٱلۡمُنذَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamtarna AAalayhim mataran fasaa mataru almunthareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: pouring upon them a rain ˹of brimstone˺. How evil was the rain of those who had been warned!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The companions of the thicket denied the messengers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The residents of the Forest rejected the messengers

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ شُعَيۡبٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala lahum shuAAaybun ala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When Shu'ayb said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when Shu’aib said to them, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍۖ إِنۡ أَجۡرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ أَوۡفُواْ ٱلۡكَيۡلَ وَلَا تَكُونُواْ مِنَ ٱلۡمُخۡسِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Awfoo alkayla wala takoonoo mina almukhsireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Give full measure and do not be of those who cause loss.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Give full measure, and cause no loss ˹to others˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And weigh with an even balance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Weigh with an even balance,

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا تَبۡخَسُواْ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشۡيَآءَهُمۡ وَلَا تَعۡثَوۡاْ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ مُفۡسِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala tabkhasoo alnnasa ashyaahum wala taAAthaw fee alardi mufsideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not deprive people of their due and do not commit abuse on earth, spreading corruption.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and do not defraud people of their property. Nor go about spreading corruption in the land.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And fear He who created you and the former creation."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And fear the One Who created you and ˹all˺ earlier peoples.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “You are simply bewitched!

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٞ مِّثۡلُنَا وَإِن نَّظُنُّكَ لَمِنَ ٱلۡكَٰذِبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama anta illa basharun mithluna wain nathunnuka lamina alkathibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You are but a man like ourselves, and indeed, we think you are among the liars.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Also, you are only a human being like us, and we think you are indeed a liar.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَسۡقِطۡ عَلَيۡنَا كِسَفٗا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faasqit AAalayna kisafan mina alssamai in kunta mina alssadiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So cause to fall upon us fragments of the sky, if you should be of the truthful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So cause ˹deadly˺ pieces of the sky to fall upon us, if what you say is true.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord is most knowing of what you do."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Shu’aib responded, “My Lord knows best whatever you do.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمۡ عَذَابُ يَوۡمِ ٱلظُّلَّةِۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَذَابَ يَوۡمٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakaththaboohu faakhathahum AAathabu yawmi alththullati innahu kana AAathaba yawmin AAatheemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they denied him, so the punishment of the day of the black cloud seized them. Indeed, it was the punishment of a terrible day.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they rejected him, and ˹so˺ were overtaken by the torment of the day of the ˹deadly˺ cloud. That was really a torment of a tremendous day.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكۡثَرُهُم مُّؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, the Qur'an is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is certainly a revelation from the Lord of all worlds,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: which the trustworthy spirit ˹Gabriel˺ brought down

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Upon your heart, [O Muhammad] - that you may be of the warners -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: into your heart ˹O Prophet˺—so that you may be one of the warners—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In a clear Arabic language.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in a clear Arabic tongue.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, it is [mentioned] in the scriptures of former peoples.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And it has indeed been ˹foretold˺ in the Scriptures of those before.

ayahs.textArabic: أَوَلَمۡ يَكُن لَّهُمۡ ءَايَةً أَن يَعۡلَمَهُۥ عُلَمَٰٓؤُاْ بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Awalam yakun lahum ayatan an yaAAlamahu AAulamao banee israeela
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And has it not been a sign to them that it is recognized by the scholars of the Children of Israel?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Was it not sufficient proof for the deniers that it has been recognized by the knowledgeable among the Children of Israel?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And even if We had revealed it to one among the foreigners
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had We revealed it to a non-Arab,

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَرَأَهُۥ عَلَيۡهِم مَّا كَانُواْ بِهِۦ مُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqaraahu AAalayhim ma kanoo bihi mumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he had recited it to them [perfectly], they would [still] not have been believers in it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who would then recite it to the deniers ˹in fluent Arabic˺, still they would not have believed in it!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thus have We inserted disbelief into the hearts of the criminals.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is how We allow denial ˹to steep˺ into the hearts of the wicked.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ بِهِۦ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُاْ ٱلۡعَذَابَ ٱلۡأَلِيمَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yuminoona bihi hatta yarawoo alAAathaba alaleema
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it will come to them suddenly while they perceive [it] not.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: which will take them by surprise when they least expect ˹it˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they will say, "May we be reprieved?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then they will cry, “Can we be allowed more time?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So for Our punishment are they impatient?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do they ˹really˺ seek to hasten Our torment?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then have you considered if We gave them enjoyment for years
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Imagine ˹O Prophet˺ if We allowed them enjoyment for years,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And then there came to them that which they were promised?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then there came to them what they had been threatened with:

ayahs.textArabic: مَآ أَغۡنَىٰ عَنۡهُم مَّا كَانُواْ يُمَتَّعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma aghna AAanhum ma kanoo yumattaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They would not be availed by the enjoyment with which they were provided.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: would that enjoyment be of any benefit to them ˹at all˺?

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَهۡلَكۡنَا مِن قَرۡيَةٍ إِلَّا لَهَا مُنذِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama ahlakna min qaryatin illa laha munthiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not destroy any city except that it had warners
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have never destroyed a society without warners

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to remind ˹them˺, for We would never wrong ˹anyone˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the devils have not brought the revelation down.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It was not the devils who brought this ˹Quran˺ down:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is not allowable for them, nor would they be able.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: it is not for them ˹to do so˺, nor can they,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed they, from [its] hearing, are removed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: for they are strictly barred from ˹even˺ overhearing ˹it˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَا تَدۡعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fala tadAAu maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara fatakoona mina almuAAaththabeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So do not invoke with Allah another deity and [thus] be among the punished.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So do not ever call upon any other god besides Allah, or you will be one of the punished.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And warn, [O Muhammad], your closest kindred.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And warn ˹all, starting with˺ your closest relatives,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱخۡفِضۡ جَنَاحَكَ لِمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waikhfid janahaka limani ittabaAAaka mina almumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And lower your wing to those who follow you of the believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and be gracious to the believers who follow you.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِنۡ عَصَوۡكَ فَقُلۡ إِنِّي بَرِيٓءٞ مِّمَّا تَعۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fain AAasawka faqul innee bareeon mimma taAAmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if they disobey you, then say, "Indeed, I am disassociated from what you are doing."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But if they disobey you, say, “I am certainly free of what you do.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And rely upon the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Put your trust in the Almighty, Most Merciful,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who sees you when you arise
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Who sees you when you rise ˹for prayer at night˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And your movement among those who prostrate.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as well as your movements ˹in prayer˺ along with ˹fellow˺ worshippers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He ˹alone˺ is indeed the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

ayahs.textArabic: هَلۡ أُنَبِّئُكُمۡ عَلَىٰ مَن تَنَزَّلُ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hal onabbiokum AAala man tanazzalu alshshayateenu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Shall I inform you of whom the devils ˹actually˺ descend upon?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They descend upon every sinful liar.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They descend upon every sinful liar,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who gives an ˹attentive˺ ear ˹to half-truths˺, mostly passing on sheer lies.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the poets - [only] the deviators follow them;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for poets, they are followed ˹merely˺ by deviants.

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَمۡ تَرَ أَنَّهُمۡ فِي كُلِّ وَادٖ يَهِيمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alam tara annahum fee kulli wadin yaheemoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do you not see that in every valley they roam
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do you not see how they rant in every field,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that they say what they do not do? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: only saying what they never do?

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَذَكَرُواْ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرٗا وَٱنتَصَرُواْ مِنۢ بَعۡدِ مَا ظُلِمُواْۗ وَسَيَعۡلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓاْ أَيَّ مُنقَلَبٖ يَنقَلِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati wathakaroo Allaha katheeran waintasaroo min baAAdi ma thulimoo wasayaAAlamu allatheena thalamoo ayya munqalabin yanqaliboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except those [poets] who believe and do righteous deeds and remember Allah often and defend [the Muslims] after they were wronged. And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Except those who believe, do good, remember Allah often, and ˹poetically˺ avenge ˹the believers˺ after being wrongfully slandered. The wrongdoers will come to know what ˹evil˺ end they will meet.
